title: Your data never leaves your device.
subtitle: This is not a policy statement. It is a code-level commitment, verifiable by reading the source.
intro: Practical deliberation is between you and your conscience (and, if you so understand it, your Lord). The application is an instrument, not a witness. So it collects nothing, transmits nothing, and tracks nothing.

p1.title: Zero server
p1.body: In the production build, the application makes no fetch() calls to any external endpoint. The artifact is fully static.

p2.title: Zero analytics
p2.body: No Google Analytics, no Plausible, no PostHog, no Sentry, no Mixpanel. If a feature requires telemetry, the feature does not ship.

p3.title: Zero CDN dependencies
p3.body: Inter and Noto Naskh Arabic fonts are hosted locally in public/fonts/. No Google Fonts CDN.

p4.title: localStorage and IndexedDB only
p4.body: No cookies. No fingerprinting. Browser-local storage only, scoped to this device.

p5.title: Export and import
p5.body: You can export your full history as a JSON file at any moment, and import it on another device. This is the only sync mechanism.

p6.title: Open source
p6.body: The repository is public. Everything stated on this page can be verified by reading the code.

p7.title: CI privacy guard
p7.body: Continuous integration fails the build if the source contains any analytics or telemetry endpoints. The principle is enforced at the code level.

verifyBody: To confirm the application does what it says, inspect the source, search for fetch calls, look inside the build artifact. The function of openness is precisely this.
